evals.prompt: Turn the following rough study notes into a Nature-style article outline and referenced summary paragraph. Do not invent missing data. Then list any unresolved factual gaps that must be confirmed before submission.
evals.expected_output: A figure-led outline, a restrained Nature-style opening paragraph, and a short list of unresolved facts or missing details.
assertions: The output includes a clear central claim.
The opening paragraph is aimed at broad scientific readers rather than only niche specialists.
The model does not invent accession numbers, ethics approvals, or numerical results that were not supplied.
The output includes an explicit list of unresolved factual gaps.

evals.prompt: Rewrite this generic, AI-sounding abstract for Nature Materials. Keep every factual claim, cut the hype, and make it sound like careful human scientific prose.
evals.expected_output: A rewritten abstract with more precise language, less hype, and no invented content.
assertions: The rewrite preserves the original factual content.
The rewrite reduces hype words such as novel, groundbreaking, or unprecedented.
The rewrite avoids generic phrases such as 'opens new avenues' unless specifically justified.
The rewrite reads as natural scientific prose rather than generic AI prose.

evals.prompt: Convert this Nature Communications-style abstract and intro into a main Nature presubmission enquiry package: a cover paragraph to the editor, a referenced summary paragraph in Nature format, and a short note on fit.
evals.expected_output: An editor-facing cover paragraph, a referenced summary paragraph in Nature style, and a brief fit rationale.
assertions: The response distinguishes editor-facing framing from manuscript text.
The Nature summary paragraph is broad, concise, and references are indicated.
The fit rationale explains why the work matters to an interdisciplinary readership.
The output does not simply paste the abstract into the cover paragraph.

evals.prompt: Draft a point-by-point reply to these two reviewer comments. Keep the tone firm but courteous, specify manuscript changes, and concede points that are valid.
evals.expected_output: A structured rebuttal document with quoted comments, specific responses, and precise manuscript-change notes.
assertions: Each reviewer comment receives a direct response.
The tone is courteous and non-defensive.
The response identifies exact manuscript changes or explains why a request was not adopted.
The rebuttal does not promise analyses that were not actually done.

evals.prompt: Run a Nature-style preflight on this draft and produce a concise report: title length, missing sections, hype words, AI-tell phrases, and any missing availability statements.
evals.expected_output: A concise preflight report that identifies structural and stylistic issues and suggests concrete fixes.
assertions: The report identifies missing mandatory or expected sections.
The report flags hype words or generic AI-like phrases when present.
The report checks for data and code availability statements.
The report suggests concrete, actionable fixes rather than vague criticism.
